name: Netty + Jetty Dual CRITICAL CVE Fix — commons flatten-maven-plugin
description: Investigated and resolved dual CRITICAL vulnerability problem in commons module: Netty CVEs from flattenDependencyMode=all promoting transitive AWS SDK deps, and Jetty CVEs from DW 5.0.1 managing Jetty 12.1.5. Solution: remove all mode + add explicit Jetty direct deps pinned at 12.1.9.

entries.summary: RESOLVED: Dual CRITICAL CVE fix — removed flattenDependencyMode=all (eliminates Netty promotion) + added 7 explicit Jetty direct deps pinned at 12.1.9 (overrides DW 5.0.1's 12.1.5 via Maven nearest-wins). Flattened POM: 214KB→10KB, 0 Netty refs, all Jetty at 12.1.9. 921 tests pass. No ParameterStoreLookup refactoring needed.
entries.detail:
Changes to commons/pom.xml:
1. Replaced flattenDependencyMode=all with explanatory comment
2. Removed ineffective Netty exclusions from cloud-sdk-aws dependency
3. Added 7 Jetty direct deps: jetty-server, jetty-http, jetty-io, jetty-util, jetty-security, jetty-session, jetty-ee10-servlet (versions inherited from parent Jetty BOM at 12.1.9)

Doc updated: Sections 11-12 appended to 2026-05-28-netty-critical.md covering Jetty version propagation analysis, failed approaches, and final solution.

entries.summary: All verification passed: 921 tests pass, 0 Netty, all Jetty 12.1.9, 10KB flattened POM
entries.detail:
Build: SUCCESS (commons + all upstream modules)
Tests: 921 run, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 skipped
Install: SUCCESS to local .m2 repo
Flattened POM validation:
  - Size: 10KB (down from 214KB)
  - Netty grep matches: 0
  - All Jetty artifacts resolve to 12.1.9

entries.summary: Section 13 added: Impact analysis confirms zero runtime loss for consumers. All 178 promoted artifacts resolve through other dependency paths. all mode was redundant.
entries.detail:
Compared all-mode (221 artifacts, 214KB) vs direct-mode (43 artifacts, 10KB) flattened POMs.

178 extra artifacts in all-mode categorized: AWS SDK v2 (43), Dropwizard (23), Jersey/Glassfish (17), Jackson (14), Apache (14), Netty (11), AWS SDK v1 (7), Logging (7), Jakarta (6), Google/Guava (6), Jetty extras (5), Other (24), Persistence (1).

Verified against booking module: ALL 178 are resolved through booking's own dependency paths (cloud-sdk-aws direct dep, dropwizard transitives from commons, etc.). Zero truly missing artifacts.

Booking Jetty: all compile-scope at 12.1.9 (from commons direct deps). Netty: test scope only (from dynamo-integration-test chain).

Conclusion: all mode was redundant for Dropwizard consumers — added no runtime value, only CVE exposure and POM bloat.

entries.summary: Sections 14-15 added: Consumer module inventory (11 modules, 3 categories) and key commands used for analysis
entries.detail:
Consumer modules with direct commons dependency (excluding visibility, oceanschedules, partner-integrator):

Upgraded + cloud-sdk-aws (4): auth (1.0.23), booking (1.0.25), booking-bridge (1.0.21), webbl (1.0.22) — zero runtime impact
Upgraded, no cloud-sdk-aws (3): db-migration (1.0.18), registration (1.0.17), tx-tracking (1.0.17) — zero impact, don't need AWS SDK
Legacy (4): bill-of-lading, rates, shipping-instruction, value-added-service (1.R.01.021) — not yet on SNAPSHOT

network does NOT depend on commons directly (only uses mercury.commons.version for dynamo-integration-test test scope).

entries.summary: Committed and pushed 841fdf5: ION-12310 fix Netty+Jetty CRITICAL CVEs, bump to 1.0.26-SNAPSHOT
entries.detail: Commit 841fdf5 pushed to origin/feature/ION-12310-commons-cloudsdk-refactoring. 3 files: pom.xml (version bump), commons/pom.xml (remove flattenDependencyMode=all + add 7 Jetty direct deps), README.md (1.0.26-SNAPSHOT section with CVE fix changelog, intro text moved from 1.0.25).
